s: Pharaonic state along the Nile; power centered near Memphis and the delta.
Old Kingdom monumental construction; Khufu pyramid among the best known.
Palace-based culture; trade network in the Bronze Age Aegean.
Anatolian great power; chariot warfare and cuneiform diplomacy.
Egypt and Hittite Empire: major chariot battle; among the first detailed battle accounts.
Mesoamerican "mother culture" along the southern Gulf: colossal art and long influence.
Phoenician colony; later Rome's great Mediterranean rival.
Achaemenid expansion; model of multi-ethnic empire.
Greek city-states resist the Persian invasions; naval battle near Athens.

t: Lysander / fall of Athenian empire (Aegospotami era)
s: Peloponnesian war ends: Spartan hegemony; shifts in Greek politics.
Classical Athenian philosophy; democracy's treatment of free speech.
Alexander vs Darius: Persian core opened to Macedon.
First imperial unification: script, measures, and roads; harsh legalism too.
Central to Christian faith: Jerusalem in multiple religious traditions.
Pax Romana: roads, law, and Mediterranean integration.
Horn of Africa state with Red Sea and Indian Ocean trade links.
Arab expansion into Syria: reshapes the Eastern Mediterranean.
Papal crowning: Carolingian renewal and links between church and monarchy.
Raids in England mark Norse "Viking age" in British historical memory.
Danish and English thrones: brief North Sea "empire" under Cnut the Great.
Battle near Hastings: William of Normandy takes England; French and Latin influence on law.
Urban II launches armed pilgrimage: centuries of East-West Christian–Muslim war memory.
Eurasian steppe state grows into a huge empire.

t: Delhi sultanate begins (Mamluk/early Islamic N. India
s: Early Turkish rule in the north: long Indo-Islamic political tradition.
End of classical Abbasid center: shock across the Islamic world.
Wealthy Sahelian state: trans-Saharan trade; griot tradition; Islam at court.
Mali ruler's pilgrimage; legendary gold spending in Egypt and the Mediterranean.
Han-ruled dynasty: Great Wall restoration; Zheng He voyages in early Ming.
Ottomans end Byzantine state; end of a thousand-year polity; gunpowder forts matter.
Opens sustained Spanish settlement in the Caribbean; Columbian exchange begins.
Transatlantic and Indian webs: silver, empires, and Columbian exchange.
Portuguese Brazil becomes center of the Atlantic enslavement system.
Wittenberg posting begins Protestant Reformation; print multiplies the debate.
Pizarro and Spanish: Inca state crisis in the high Andes.
Japan unified under Tokugawa; Edo (Tokyo) as power base for centuries.
First permanent English North American colony; tobacco economy and contact with Algonquian peoples.
Chattel slavery in English N. America takes shape (dates debated; educational anchor).
Conquest and integration of the Ming world by Manchu-led Qing; long imperial form.
Parliament and Dutch stadtholder William: limits on the crown; Bill of Rights follow.
Thirteen colonies assert separation; republic model in the Americas.
British convict and settler colonization: beginning of large-scale change in Australia.
Crisis of monarchy becomes mass revolution: ideas spread in Europe and Americas.
World's first Black republic: Atlantic slavery and revolution in the Caribbean.
Napoleon's final defeat: post-Napoleonic order shaped at Vienna before.
Southern independences from Spain; state-building in the Andes.
Liberal and nationalist uprisings across much of Europe; mostly repressed, long legacy.
Forced open ports: catalyst for Meiji state-building.
Civil war turning point: Lincoln frames Union war as for equality.
Peaceful secession: Scandinavian union dissolved.
Failed Allied attempt on the straits: national myth in ANZ and Turkey.
Roosevelt, Churchill, Stalin plan post-WWII Europe: Cold War lines foreshadowed.
India and Pakistan independence: mass migration; Kashmir line.
State declaration and large displacement: long conflict in the region.
Vietminh defeat of French: end of Indochina war phase; U.S. deeper involvement after.
Non-Aligned: decolonized states meet; Cold War "third way" imagery.
Anglo–French–Israeli action over canal; U.S. financial pressure: decolonization and Cold War.
Many new states: Congo and others; end of many colonial rules.
Soviet missiles in Cuba: closest brush with nuclear war in Cold War story.
Civil rights mass action: "I have a dream" in national memory.
Rapid border shifts: Sinai, Golan, West Bank, and Jerusalem in dispute since.
East Pakistan secedes after conflict and Indian intervention; South Asian rebalancing.
U.K.–Argentina over islands; air–sea–land conflict in South Atlantic cold war.
Eastern Bloc opening; end of a divided city as Cold War symbol.
1989: student and worker protests: global image of China's 1980s opening.
Coalition after Iraqi invasion: large U.S. ground presence in the Gulf.
All-race election; Nelson Mandela as president; negotiated transition.
Mass violence: UN failure and post-1994 justice debates.
"One country, two systems" begins; end of British lease.
Al-Qaeda attacks: security, privacy, and Middle East policy reshape.
Regime change; WMD claim controversy; long insurgency and regional shift.
Indonesia quake: waves across the Bay of Bengal; large death toll; warning systems improve.
China as global host: soft power, surveillance, and human rights debates.
Earthquake and tsunami: nuclear safety debate and energy policy shift.
Civil airliner shot down: conflict in Donbas and international law.

t: Rio de Janeiro summer Olympics (Brazil host)
s: First South American Olympics: infrastructure, Zika, and crime in global news.
Terror against mosques: online radicalization and national gun law response.
Lockdowns, remote school, mRNA vaccine push: defining early 2020s.
